untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of: (1) climax moves defined as 'extremely large momentum move out of all proportion to recent history' - requires statistical deviation analysis or volatility percentile ranking not available in indicator set; (2) MACD rescaling behavior defined as 'recent history compressed into tight wiggly line' - requires analysis of MACD volatility/range compression relative to historical periods, which cannot be expressed with basic comparison operators on single MACD values. The signal is fundamentally a FILTER/AVOIDANCE rule (do NOT enter) rather than a directional entry signal, and the quantitative thresholds for 'climax' and 'rescaling' require multi-period statistical measures unavailable in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: This is a contrarian reversion signal warning against trend continuation entries. Core logic: (1) Identify extreme momentum event (climax) relative to recent history - requires volatility ratio or Z-score; (2) Detect MACD compression/rescaling - requires measuring MACD range compression over rolling windows. Neither can be expressed with point-in-time indicator comparisons. Recommendation: Implement as custom indicator checking (atr_14 or hvol_20 percentile rank > 90th) for climax, and (macd recent_range / historical_range < threshold) for rescaling.
